metadata.compiled_for: World History Simulator for Fiction Generation

cards.title: Dwarf Fortress (Wikipedia overview)
cards.key_claim: DF generates a fantasy world via 1000-year procedural history step where civilizations interact, then preserves that world as the substrate for Fortress and Adventurer modes. Two-decade development; gold standard of agent-driven historical simulation.
cards.relevance: Core reference for world-gen->legends->play pipeline architecture. Our 'tick world state continuously' is the same paradigm.

cards.title: Bay 12 Games dev story / Threetoe stories
cards.key_claim: Zach 'Threetoe' Adams writes short fiction that he and Tarn analyse to extract simulation primitives. Stories drive feature design rather than scripts.
cards.relevance: Anti-pattern check: do not over-script. Direct evidence that story->mechanic extraction works long-term.

cards.title: ThreeToe page (Dwarf Fortress Wiki)
cards.key_claim: Threetoe's stories are explicitly 'used to drive future development' via post-story analysis listings.
cards.relevance: Confirms the fiction-first design loop.

cards.title: DF2014 Legends mode (DF Wiki)
cards.key_claim: Legends mode exposes the simulation as queryable history: characters, civs, conflicts, artifacts, books-of-books recorded as causal sentences.
cards.relevance: Direct analog to our 'canon stages' - narrative is a side-effect of simulation, not the simulation.

cards.title: Procedural Humanities: Interview with Tarn Adams (HASTAC, 2010)
cards.key_claim: Adams' design vision: 'we don't want another cheap fantasy universe - we want a cheap fantasy universe generator.' Roadmap targets emergent narrative + procedural culture as terminal goal.
cards.relevance: Philosophical anchor: distinguishes simulator-as-substrate vs simulator-as-author.

cards.title: Recurse Games: Generating Procedural Myths and Histories
cards.key_claim: Adams' myth generator seeds with one entity (god/cosmic egg) and grows mythology by random spawning + interaction events; mythology is a list of (entity,interaction) tuples.
cards.relevance: Direct template for Bazi-cosmology layer: seed deterministic priors, then step.

cards.title: Tarn Adams on simulating magic systems (PC Gamer)
cards.key_claim: Adams targets a magic system with deeper simulation than any existing system; cosmology is procedurally generated per world.
cards.relevance: Per-world cosmology is also our target (Bazi/Qimen are per-protagonist priors).

cards.title: RimWorld AI Storytellers (RimWorld Wiki)
cards.key_claim: Three storyteller personalities - Cassandra (rising tension), Phoebe (chillax/peace), Randy (chaos) - drive event scheduling. The storyteller is a meta-narrative agent on top of simulation.
cards.relevance: Direct match for our 'consistency-gate scoring' + author-decision-needed pause: the storyteller is the gating layer.

cards.title: The Story Generator: Game Design Analysis of RimWorld (Substack)
cards.key_claim: Sylvester (designer) structures RW as a 'story generator' - simulation feeds storyteller, storyteller arms triggers, output is unique-to-playthrough drama.
cards.relevance: Architectural pattern: separate sim from drama-manager from output renderer.

cards.title: RimWorld vs DF: procedurally generated storytelling (Game Developer)
cards.key_claim: DF is bottom-up emergence; RW is top-down storyteller-curated emergence. Both produce 'stories' but RW is more legible.
cards.relevance: Critical decision point: pure emergence (DF) vs author-aware curation (RW).

cards.title: CK3 Traits (Paradox Wiki)
cards.key_claim: Trait system: congenital, personality, education, lifestyle. Congenital traits inheritable with leveling chains (Attractiveness/Intelligence/Strength six-tier ladders).
cards.relevance: Reference architecture for 'Bazi as inherited prior + acquired modifiers' character model.

cards.title: CK3 Stress / Mental Break (Paradox Wiki)
cards.key_claim: Stress accumulates when actions go against trait nature; mental breaks fire trait-specific events (wrathful->yelling at L1, murdering heir at L3).
cards.relevance: Tension-budget mechanic for forcing dramatic events. Excellent prior-violation cost model.

cards.title: Like Sands Through The Hour Glass: CK3 Emergent Narrative (Paste)
cards.key_claim: CK3's procedural narrative is an asset because of its uncontrollability; combinatorial trait permutations make every dynasty feel novelistic.
cards.relevance: Validation: trait-density + small randomness = novel-grade narrative.

cards.title: Dev Diary #31: A Stressful Situation (Paradox Forum)
cards.key_claim: Designers explicitly describe stress as the bridge between trait personality and gameplay; 'forcing characters to act against nature' is the core dramatic primitive.
cards.relevance: Steal: explicit prior-violation accounting.

cards.title: Subverting Historical Cause & Effect: Mythic Biographies in Caves of Qud (Grinblat & Bucklew, FDG'17)
cards.key_claim: Sultan biographies: state-machine drives random sultan actions, then a 'sifting' pattern infers post-hoc motivations. 'History is what you remember, biased by retelling.'
cards.relevance: CRUCIAL: post-hoc motivation inference is the right mental model for retroactively rationalizing simulator output into novel-grade text.

cards.title: Caves of Qud (Wikipedia)
cards.key_claim: Sultans + stratified ruins + Qudpedia: each playthrough has a five-Sultan history with conflicting accounts and parchment fragments hidden in dungeons.
cards.relevance: Multi-source canon = 'each NPC's truth differs from absolute history.' Match for our consistency gate that allows POV variants.

cards.title: Math for Game Devs: End-to-End Procedural Generation in Caves of Qud (GDC Vault)
cards.key_claim: Bucklew explains the pipeline: world->history->civilizations->artifacts->dungeons->lore-fragments, all procedural. Each layer constrains the next.
cards.relevance: Layered procedural pipeline pattern.

cards.title: Generative Agents: Interactive Simulacra of Human Behavior (Park et al, UIST'23)
cards.key_claim: 25-agent Smallville with memory-stream + reflection + planning. Agents form opinions, plan days, gossip, throw a Valentine's Day party emergently.
cards.relevance: Foundational LLM-agent architecture. Memory stream + retrieval + reflection IS the canonical pattern we need.

cards.title: Stanford HAI: Computational Agents Exhibit Believable Humanlike Behavior
cards.key_claim: Ablation shows all three components (memory, reflection, planning) needed; removing any degrades believability sharply.
cards.relevance: Don't shortcut: all three layers are load-bearing.

cards.title: Generative Agents repository (GitHub joonspk-research)
cards.key_claim: Open-source Smallville code: scheduler, perception, retrieval, prompt templates, sandbox.
cards.relevance: Direct reference implementation we can fork/study.

cards.title: Park et al. dissertation: 1000-agent simulation of US adults
cards.key_claim: Park's dissertation extends to 1000-agent population sim of US adults; documents alignment failure modes at scale.
cards.relevance: Scaling beyond 25 is doable but requires fidelity instrumentation.

cards.title: Project Sid: Many-agent simulations toward AI civilization (Altera, arxiv 2411.00114)
cards.key_claim: PIANO architecture: parallel info aggregation. Up to 1000 agents in Minecraft self-organize into roles, governance, religion, taxation, cultural memes.
cards.relevance: Demonstrates large-scale multi-agent civilization is feasible. Our character-graph approach must scale similarly when protagonist meets 100+ NPCs.

cards.title: Project Sid GitHub (altera-al)
cards.key_claim: Open-source Sid release including agent code, memory primitives.
cards.relevance: Reference implementation for many-agent.

cards.title: Voyager: An Open-Ended Embodied Agent (Wang et al, arxiv 2305.16291)
cards.key_claim: GPT-4 Minecraft agent with three components: automatic curriculum + skill library (executable code) + iterative self-verification prompting. 3.3x more items, 15.3x faster tech-tree.
cards.relevance: Skill library = re-usable narrative capabilities. We can store 'narrative moves' similarly.

cards.title: Agents: Open-source Framework for Autonomous Language Agents (Zhou et al, arxiv 2309.07870)
cards.key_claim: Modular framework: planning, memory, tool use, multi-agent comm, fine-grained symbolic control. Designed for non-specialists.
cards.relevance: Off-the-shelf scaffolding option.

cards.title: AutoGen: Multi-Agent Conversation Framework (Wu et al, arxiv 2308.08155)
cards.key_claim: Conversational multi-agent framework. v0.4 is async/event-driven. Now in maintenance mode in favor of MS Agent Framework.
cards.relevance: Reference for async event-driven multi-agent infrastructure.

cards.title: MetaAgents: LLM agents for decision-making on teaming (arxiv 2310.06500)
cards.key_claim: Job-fair simulation: LLM agents form teams via dialogue + role alignment. Performance degrades with participant count due to misalignment.
cards.relevance: Cautionary: persona-role alignment is fragile at scale.

cards.title: Facade: Fully-Realized Interactive Drama (Mateas & Stern, GDC'03)
cards.key_claim: Drama manager sequences hand-authored 'beats' in response to player input. Joint dialog behaviors written in ABL reactive-planning language.
cards.relevance: Beat-based drama-management is the canonical alternative to free emergence.

cards.title: Structuring Content in the Facade Architecture (Mateas, AIIDE'05)
cards.key_claim: Beats are story-atoms with preconditions, behaviors, effects on dramatic tension; drama manager queries beats and selects via tension curve.
cards.relevance: Steal: tension-budget gates = consistency-gate.

cards.title: Versu - A Simulationist Storytelling System (Evans & Short, 2014)
cards.key_claim: Versu: autonomous social agents + 'social practices' (norms) + storylets. Players form unscripted relationships because the social model is generative.
cards.relevance: Direct template for protagonist-centered relationship graph that supports unscripted bonds.

cards.title: Introducing Versu (Emily Short blog)
cards.key_claim: Short documents the design philosophy: 'because the social model is generative, you can have romance the author never wrote.'
cards.relevance: Crystallizes: social-physics > scripted scenes.

cards.title: Ink: inkle's narrative scripting language
cards.key_claim: Ink: declarative scripting for branching narrative + variable state + knot-stitch-divert primitives + external function calls.
cards.relevance: Industry-standard DSL for narrative; our author-decision system could compile to ink-like form.

cards.title: Heaven's Vault: how Inkle wrote its own herstory (MCV/DEVELOP)
cards.key_claim: Heaven's Vault uses 'narrative tweening': mix-and-match conversational atoms fill gaps between keyframes. Camera/scene driven by ink.
cards.relevance: Tweening pattern for our 'canon stage' boundary text.

cards.title: Mythic Game Master Emulator (Word Mill Games)
cards.key_claim: Fate Chart (13x13) maps 'likelihood' x 'chaos factor' to yes/no/exceptional with random events; Meaning Tables (47 in 2nd ed) supply narrative detail.
cards.relevance: Direct analog: Bazi/Qimen as our Fate Chart. The chaos-factor knob is the master tension dial.

cards.title: Free Oracle: Mythic Fate Chart v1 (Fantasy Grounds blog)
cards.key_claim: Detailed Fate Chart layout: rows=action rank, cols=difficulty, cells=percent chance. Chaos modifies all rolls per scene.
cards.relevance: Probability-table-as-prior reference.

cards.title: Ironsworn Oracles (Tomkin Press)
cards.key_claim: 'Ask the Oracle' move: binary 'likely/unlikely' table + open-ended inspiration tables. Oracle answers feed into narrative as facts.
cards.relevance: Lean oracle pattern; cleaner than Mythic; Bazi/Qimen produce richer answers.

cards.title: Tarot-based narrative generation (Sullivan, FDG'18)
cards.key_claim: Tarot-card draws -> templated movie-synopsis generator. Symbolic prior + NLP rendering.
cards.relevance: Direct precedent for divination-prior-driven narrative gen.

cards.title: Tarot as Procedural Storytelling (Manning, in 'Procedural Storytelling in Game Design')
cards.key_claim: Tarot is a 'compressed dictionary of human archetypes' - random draw is a controllable randomness source for storytelling.
cards.relevance: Cosmological priors as compression of narrative archetypes - same principle as Bazi.

cards.title: Procedural Storytelling in Game Design (Short & Adams, eds, 2019)
cards.key_claim: 33-essay anthology: characterization, world-building, theme, momentum from generative perspective. Edited by Tanya Short and Tarn Adams.
cards.relevance: Master canonical reference for the field.

cards.title: Pendragon (TTRPG, Wikipedia)
cards.key_claim: Pendragon's Winter Phase: between adventures, characters age, marry, raise heirs, manage estates; 82-year campaign requires generational succession.
cards.relevance: Direct template for 'months of runtime' world-tick where most ticks are non-dramatic state evolution.

cards.title: Great Pendragon Campaign (Chaosium)
cards.key_claim: 432-page campaign book: detailed year-by-year events 485-end-of-Arthur. Demonstrates how a fixed historical skeleton plus character agency produces 30-year campaigns.
cards.relevance: Steal: historical-skeleton + per-character improvisation.

cards.title: Burning Wheel: Beliefs and Instincts (publisher page)
cards.key_claim: BITs system: Belief = strategic goal, Instinct = automatic behavior, Trait = identity descriptor. Artha (Fate/Persona/Deeds) rewards engaging with BITs.
cards.relevance: Steal: Belief = trigger that obligates GM/sim to test it. Direct mechanism for 'obligated dramatic events'.

cards.title: Four Pillars of Destiny (Wikipedia)
cards.key_claim: BaZi maps year/month/day/hour to 8 stem-branch chars via 60-year sexagenary cycle (Han dynasty origin); modern Ziping method since Song dynasty.
cards.relevance: Deterministic algorithm spec.

cards.title: BaZi-Based Character Simulation Benchmark (Zheng et al, arxiv 2510.23337)
cards.key_claim: First QA dataset for BaZi persona reasoning. BaZi-LLM system integrates symbolic reasoning + LLM. 30.3-62.6% accuracy gain over DeepSeek-v3/GPT-5-mini. Wrong BaZi -> 20-45% accuracy drop, proving the symbolic prior is load-bearing.
cards.relevance: DEFINITIVE evidence that BaZi-as-prior actually improves character simulation in LLM. Direct citation for our architecture.

cards.title: BaziQA-Benchmark: Symbolic + Temporal Reasoning in LLMs (arxiv 2602.12889)
cards.key_claim: Companion benchmark evaluating LLMs on symbolic-and-temporally-compositional BaZi reasoning.
cards.relevance: Eval harness reference.

cards.title: Cantian AI / bazi-mcp (GitHub)
cards.key_claim: MCP server providing deterministic BaZi computation (handles DST, true solar time, lunar-solar conversion).
cards.relevance: Reference implementation we can adopt as the BaZi calculation backbone.

cards.title: Qimen Dunjia (Wikipedia)
cards.key_claim: 9-palace chart with 9 stars / 8 gates / 8 gods / 10 stems mapping per 2-hour interval. Originally military strategy / statecraft system.
cards.relevance: Algorithm spec for Qimen layer.

cards.title: QiAdvisor / Master Sean Chan / Joey Yap calculators
cards.key_claim: Multiple deterministic calculator implementations exist; algorithm fully specified.
cards.relevance: Confirms algorithmic tractability.

cards.title: SCORE: Story Coherence and Retrieval Enhancement for AI Narratives (arxiv 2503.23512)
cards.key_claim: Hybrid: dynamic state tracking + context-aware summarization + TF-IDF + semantic embedding retrieval. +23.6% coherence (NCI-2.0), 89.7% emotional consistency (EASM), -41.8% hallucinations vs baseline GPT.
cards.relevance: Direct numerical baseline for consistency-gate metrics.

cards.title: Lost in Stories: Consistency Bugs in Long Story Generation (arxiv 2603.05890)
cards.key_claim: ConStory-Bench: 5 error categories, 19 subtypes. Quantifies consistency degradation as length scales.
cards.relevance: Eval benchmark for our consistency gate.

cards.title: Can LLMs Generate Good Stories? Narrative Planning Perspective (arxiv 2506.10161)
cards.key_claim: LLMs fail at long-horizon narrative planning without explicit goal/state tracking. Plot-skeleton outperforms free generation.
cards.relevance: Argues for our skeleton + tick + render architecture.

cards.title: SNAP: Plan-Driven Framework for Controllable Interactive Narrative (arxiv 2601.11529)
cards.key_claim: Confines context within 'Cells' with explicit plans (spatiotemporal setting + character action + plot dev), preventing drift.
cards.relevance: Cell-based context-management pattern; matches our 'canon stage' chunking.

cards.title: Patchview: LLM-Powered Worldbuilding with Generative Dust (arxiv 2408.04112)
cards.key_claim: Visual+LLM worldbuilding tool with 'dust' (atomic facts) and 'magnets' (organizing concepts). Designed for author co-creation.
cards.relevance: UI pattern for author-decision loop.

cards.title: Drama Llama: LLM-Powered Storylets Framework (arxiv 2501.09099)
cards.key_claim: Storylets with natural-language triggers fired by LLM. Authors define triggers in NL, no DSL programming needed.
cards.relevance: Steal: NL-trigger storylets layer above simulation.

cards.title: Open-Theatre: Open-Source Toolkit for LLM-based Interactive Drama (arxiv 2509.16713)
cards.key_claim: Director-Global-Actor framework for LLM drama. Director schedules, GlobalActor maintains world consistency, Actors play roles.
cards.relevance: Direct architectural blueprint for our system.

cards.title: what-if: Branching Narratives via Meta-Prompting LLMs (arxiv 2412.10582)
cards.key_claim: Zero-shot meta-prompting generates coherent branches at major plot decision points from a linear prewritten story.
cards.relevance: Direct method for our 'branch alternatives' generation.

cards.title: A Survey on LLMs for Story Generation (Findings of EMNLP 2025)
cards.key_claim: Comprehensive 2025 survey of LLM story-gen field: planning, controllability, consistency, evaluation. Identifies long-form fiction as a major open frontier.
cards.relevance: Field-level overview confirming fiction-gen+sim is still green field.

cards.title: Beyond the Context Window: Architecting Long-Form Story Generation (Pratilipi engineering blog, Feb 2026)
cards.key_claim: 'Long-form generation isn't a prompting problem; it's a systems engineering problem.' Stories as stateful systems with constraints, contracts, memory.
cards.relevance: Industry validation: our architectural framing is correct.

cards.title: Book-Agent: Epistemically Controlled Long-Form Fiction Pipeline (Level1Techs)
cards.key_claim: Maintains per-character knowledge state across timelines; emotional arcs, parallel plot threads.
cards.relevance: Hobbyist confirmation of multi-character POV bookkeeping.

cards.title: NovelAI Lorebook architecture documentation
cards.key_claim: Lorebook: dynamic context injection of worldbuilding entries based on text triggers; Story Bible (Sudowrite) is parallel concept.
cards.relevance: Industry pattern for selective context injection - matches our 'character set is dynamic' need.

cards.title: Character.AI PipSqueak 2 + Lorebook infrastructure shift
cards.key_claim: Character.AI rolling out persistent memory + lorebook to address character drift; explicit acknowledgment that long-context alone is insufficient.
cards.relevance: Industry-scale validation of memory+lorebook pattern.
